one_thing_to_fix.headline: Fear Anchor — When She Opens the Door, Walk Through It
one_thing_to_fix.detail: Jen revealed two fear moments: (1) door punch + 'something underlying' and (2) 'what if we don't do something now, I'm totally untamed.' Both times Bailey acknowledged generically and moved on. The fix: when a parent reveals something heavy, STOP. Repeat her exact words. Ask ONE follow-up: 'What went through your mind when you saw that?' or 'What does untamed look like to you?'
one_thing_to_fix.ceiling: Without fixing E: 5.0 ceiling. With E at 2+: 6.5+ because structure is now there.

coaching_history.drill: FEAR ANCHOR DRILL: Review Jen Howgate transcript. Find the door punch moment. Practice: 'Jen, when you saw that hole in the door — what went through you as his mum?' Say it 5 times. Then find the 'untamed' moment. Practice: 'You said untamed — what does that look like for you?' 5 times. The discipline: when she reveals something heavy, STOP talking about yourself. Ask about HER.

coaching_history.pattern: STRONG FOUNDATION, MISSED $111 CLOSE SIGNAL. Emily revealed DV context, Charlie's anger pattern mirroring abusive father, no male role models, gaming isolation. Tom held space well in discovery, landed the fear question, and reflected the 'becoming his dad' revelation accurately. The call was well-built — the gap was at the very end: Emily said 'I'm in sales, I'm keen to do it' + 'I'll make it work' while Tom had already pivoted to Tuesday. The $111 (not $2,700) close was right there — Emily had isolated to cash flow only, confirmed she was keen, and Tom accepted the follow-up instead of attempting the $111 on-call. Tuesday re-anchor locked in for April 7.
coaching_history.the_moment: Emily said 'Don't worry about doing a closing pitch. I'm in sales. I'm keen to do it.' — That is a YES on the $111. Tom responded: 'Yeah cool. Because I definitely think it's worth it. Awesome.' and moved toward Tuesday booking. One question — 'The $111 is literally $111, not $2,700 — is that doable today?' — would have closed this on-call.
key_strengths: Fear Q landed perfectly — Emily revealed 'I'm worried he's going to end up treating a woman this way / becoming his dad' — one of the clearest fear revelations scored this month
Tom's reflection of the fear: 'it does sound like you're a little bit worried that he might end up like his dad one day' — got explicit confirmation, stayed with it before moving on
Dentist metaphor + gaming/Fortnite context for son buy-in was perfectly personalised to Charlie
Isolation was clean: 'apart from the investment, if we sorted the money piece, are you ready to go forward?' — got YES
Tuesday follow-up booked before hanging up — professional close of a difficult financial situation

one_thing_to_fix.headline: Small YES Close — When They Say 'I'm Keen,' Ask for the $111
one_thing_to_fix.detail: Emily said 'I'm in sales, I'm keen to do it' and 'I'll make it work.' This is a buying signal on the $111. The $111 is not $2,700. Tom had already isolated price as the only blocker and got a YES on value. The next line is: 'Emily, I hear you on the week — and the $111 is literally just $111, not the big number. It just reserves Charlie's spot. Is that doable today?' You don't need to be pushy — just ask once specifically for the small commitment before accepting the follow-up. The Tuesday booking is great as a fallback. But this was closeable on-call.
one_thing_to_fix.ceiling: This pattern (single-mum financial concern + genuine keenness) will come up regularly. Without the $111 close attempt: follow-up risk. With it: likely closes same call. Ceiling lifts from 6.8 to 8+ on this call profile.

coaching_history.drill: SMALL YES DRILL: When the parent has isolated to cash flow only (not value), confirmed they're keen, and the $111 frame has been delivered — ask once: 'The $111 is literally just $111 — is that doable today?' Practice: role-play 5 scenarios where the parent says 'I'm keen but not this week.' Force yourself to ask specifically about the $111 before booking the follow-up. If they still say no, book the follow-up. One extra question, one extra chance.
rye_coaching_role: Tuesday April 7 pre-call debrief: what's Tom's opening line when Emily picks up? Must anchor on the DV/becoming his dad fear, NOT on the program
Post-call debrief: did Tom attempt the $111 close before hanging up?
Run Small Yes Close drill 3x this week
Review Emily Brady transcript together — find the 'I'm in sales' moment

weekly_focus.headline: The $111 Is Not $2,700 — Ask For It
weekly_focus.detail: When a parent isolates to cash flow only and confirms value — the $111 close is still available. One extra question before the follow-up booking. That's the drill.

coaching_history.key_learning: The fear question landed perfectly. The discovery was strong. The gap was at the end — not recognising that 'I'm in sales, I'm keen' is a small YES signal that justifies one more attempt at the $111 before accepting a future date.

objections_faced.better_line: Emily, I hear you on the week. The $111 is literally $111 — not the big number. It just reserves Charlie's spot and gets his mentor match started. You said you'll make it work — is the $111 doable today?

coaching_history.pattern: EMERGING — Low-urgency family handling. Bec Richardson (high urgency, single mum) = 7.5. Elisabeth Poole (engaged two-parent family, son not at risk) = 5.2. The single-urgency deepening script doesn't work when the family is functioning well and already knows what they need.
coaching_history.the_moment: Jason was on the call. Elizabeth said "Let me chat to Jason and myself." JASON WAS THERE. Tom's response: "Yeah of course, completely understand." The close was in that room. One question — "Jason, from what you've heard, do you feel like this is what Michael needs?" — and it likely closes.
coaching_history.drill: BOTH-PARENTS CLOSE DRILL: When both parents are on a call, after mum speaks — turn to dad. "Jason/[Dad], from what you've heard today — do you feel like this is what [son] needs?" Get his YES before she can defer to him as a blocker. Practice 5 times. This is the most common missed close on engaged two-parent families.
coaching_history.metric_to_watch: OH Score — dropped from 4 to 2. Also E Score — 2.0 avg. Watch these together. Target: E=3+, OH=3+ by call 4.

When the call ended without close, the 'think about it' hardened into a Price Objection. The Elisabeth pattern = most dangerous non-close outcome: engaged two-parent family, son not at crisis, dad silent on call. Tom must turn to dad before she can defer. Emily Brady still pending — Tuesday follow-up is critical re-anchor call.
coaching_history.the_moment: Elisabeth Poole: 'Let me chat to Jason and myself.' Jason had been on the call the entire time. Tom said: 'Yeah of course, completely understand.' The close was right there — one question: 'Jason, from what you've heard today, does this feel like what Michael needs?' Not asked. Outcome hardened to Call Objection.

Online objection handled with precision — Xbox/Roblox personalised, COVID reframe used appropriately

one_thing_to_fix.headline: Name Carryover Error + Past-Failure Question
one_thing_to_fix.detail: Mid-pitch Tom called Riley 'Samuel' — Samuel was Lorissa Garcia's son from the previous day. This is a preparation failure: write the son's name on paper before every call and reference it 3x in the first 5 minutes. Separately: Kristy handed Tom the past-failure gold standard setup ('fought for years, lasted 6 months') — Harry's question 'What do you think missed the mark with previous supports?' was never asked. This question alone could have lifted the call from 6.2 to 7.5+.

'Jason/[Dad], you've been listening — from your perspective, does this sound like what [son] needs right now?' Practice 5x. This is a different close from the absent-partner sequence (Anthony/Judith). Dad is IN the room. Use him.

one_thing_to_fix.headline: Ask For The $111 ONCE Before Accepting Any Deferral
one_thing_to_fix.detail: Third consecutive call accepting a deferral without attempting the $111 close. Rebecca was NDIS-funded, warm, sole decision-maker, asked about invoicing proactively. 'I get paid Saturday' is not a no — it's a timing constraint on a $111 amount. One question: 'The $111 is literally just $111 — is that doable today?' If still no, accept gracefully.
one_thing_to_fix.ceiling: Without fixing this: 6.5 ceiling on warm leads. With it fixed: 8+ because everything else is already there.

Say it out loud 5 times. Then: review Rebecca Smith transcript, find the deferral moment, rehearse the line. The discipline is asking ONCE. Not twice. Not pushing.

weekly_focus.headline: ONE Question Before Every Follow-Up Booking
weekly_focus.detail: Three consecutive deferrals accepted without the $111 question. The drill is mechanical: warm lead + deferral = ask 'The $111 is literally just $111 — is that doable today?' ONCE. Then accept gracefully.

Tom builds excellent calls — the emotional foundation is there, the pitch is personalised, the value is established. But he's leaving the close on the table by accepting every deferral without one clean attempt at the small commitment.

But THREE critical moments missed: (1) 'I don't want to visit him in Long Bay Jail' — JAIL BOMB walked past with 'Yeah definitely'. (2) George was at the psych with Anthony RIGHT NOW — perfect urgency moment unused ('George is already investing in Anthony today'). (3) Kirian said 7-8/10 value but 'I'd never give anybody a ten' — Tom accepted this instead of using it as a bridge to the small commitment.

George was at the psychologist with Anthony at that exact moment. The line was: 'Kirian, George is literally at the psych with Anthony right now — he's already invested. The $111 just gets the mentor match started alongside what the psych is doing. Is that doable today?' Not asked.

Is that doable so his mentor can reach out this week?' This is now pattern, not a one-off.
